title: ranunculus pegaeus var. pegaeus
intro: Ranunculus pegaeus var. pegaeus, commonly known as the sea ranunculus, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant's roots, rhizomes, and leaves contain glycosides and other compounds that exhibit medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus pegaeus var. pegaeus include its potential use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used externally to promote healing and soothe skin irritations.
